Electrostatic generator. No information
of the facility in the article, probably, Van de Graaf
Thick-target technique was used for
observation angles greater or equal 70. degr.
uncertainty in the spectrometer constants;
uncertainty in the effective stopping power;
uncertainty in the target angles;
the integrator calibration;
uncertainty in counting statistics (generally
about 3%)
corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases.
SD: Corrections in all Subents according to
new EXFOR rules. BIB updated. EN-ERR -> EN-ERR-DIG.
Numerical data revised.
At theta greater 70 degr. the target was a
3/4in.x3/4in. in plate of metallic beryllium about
1/8 in. thick.
For observation angles forward of 70 degr., a thin
(10 keV) layer of metallic beryllium, evaporated onto
a thin (50 keV) Cu foil, was used.
fig 1,2 from Phys.Rev.,104(1956)1307

At theta greater 70 degr. the target was a

fig.5 from Phys.Rev.,104(1956)1307
SD: SF5=PAR added. SF9=EXP deleted from
REACTION code. DATA-ERR2 -> ERR-DIG.
An unsupported Be foil was used, which was thin
enough to give a line profile for the deuteron group
but thick enough to give a step profile for the alpha
particles.
The thick-target technique and integral biasing
could be used, since the tail of the deuteron peak did
not interfere with the alpha profile.
fig 6 from Phys.Rev.,104(1956)1307
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
